title: Chicken Alfredo

overview.description: Tender chicken in a rich Parmesan and cream sauce with fettuccine. Comfort food at its best and a reliable after-work favourite.

method: Cook the pasta until al dente.
Meanwhile, season the chicken lightly with salt and pepper and cook in the butter until golden and cooked through. Add the garlic and cook for about a minute.
Pour in the double cream and bring to a gentle simmer. Stir in the Parmesan until fully melted into the sauce.
Add the cooked pasta together with a splash of pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce. Toss until every strand is coated.
Finish with plenty of freshly ground black pepper and serve immediately.

learning.did-you-know: The original Alfredo from Rome didn't contain cream. The cream-based version became popular in North America before spreading around the world.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How Parmesan thickens a cream sauce and why gentle heat keeps it smooth.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Quick, comforting and made almost entirely from ingredients we often have in the fridge.
chapman-tips: Never boil the sauce after adding the Parmesan.
A splash of pasta water keeps the sauce silky rather than heavy.
Fresh parsley is a lovely addition if you have some.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently with a splash of milk.
